preamble: The shift from the mid-back rounded vowel [o] to the labiodental fricative [v] is a form of consonantalization. It typically occurs as a strategy for hiatus resolution, where an unstressed back vowel weakens into a labio-velar glide [w] and then strengthens into an obstruent to bridge the gap between two syllables.

examples.note: Hiatus resolution where the unstressed back vowel strengthened into a labiodental fricative.
In rapid speech, the unstressed vowel in hiatus is realized as a full labiodental consonant.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
